Follow us:

Arts & Culture

Increasing access to diverse arts, cultural, and heritage experiences for everyone.
Photo courtesy Shunpike
Performers with Mariachi Noroeste, from the Icicle Creek Center for the Arts in Leavenworth, Wash. (Robert Inn Photo)

Our Program

Arts and cultural experiences contribute to a deeper understanding of ourselves and others, helping to foster connection and strengthen communities.

We believe that in order for arts and culture to flourish; artists and culture bearers must have the resources needed to exercise their craft and creativity, organizations must have the capacity and infrastructure to present, support, and deliver experiences to a broad range of diverse audiences, and the public must value and commit to the preservation of artistic and cultural experiences.     

Our Program

Arts and cultural experiences contribute to a deeper understanding of ourselves and others, helping to foster connection and strengthen communities.

We believe that in order for arts and culture to flourish; artists and culture bearers must have the resources needed to exercise their craft and creativity, organizations must have the capacity and infrastructure to present, support, and deliver experiences to a broad range of diverse audiences, and the public must value and commit to the preservation of artistic and cultural experiences.     

Performers with Mariachi Noroeste, from the Icicle Creek Center for the Arts in Leavenworth, Wash. (Robert Inn Photo)

Our Focus Areas

Supporting artists and culture bearers

Bolstering capacity and infrastructure

Strengthening public commitment and sentiment

Photo courtesy Carlin Ma/Seattle Symphony
Photo courtesy Carlin Ma/Seattle Symphony

Our focus areas center the human experience of art.  

Supporting artists and culture bearers. We make grants to provide services, working spaces, performance venues, and professional development opportunities to creators, particularly those whose voices and perspectives have been underrepresented.  

Bolstering capacity and infrastructure. We help organizations improve their long-term operating capacity and financial stability through cross-organization partnerships, cohort-based learning, community-based networks, and other new models.  

Strengthening public commitment and sentiment. We fund research and advocacy efforts to equip the creative sector with messages and tools to help them expand their audiences, increase public funding, and move arts and culture higher on the policy agenda.  

Our focus areas center the human experience of art.  

Supporting artists and culture bearers. We make grants to provide services, working spaces, performance venues, and professional development opportunities to creators, particularly those whose voices and perspectives have been underrepresented.  

Bolstering capacity and infrastructure. We help organizations improve their long-term operating capacity and financial stability through cross-organization partnerships, cohort-based learning, community-based networks, and other new models.  

Strengthening public commitment and sentiment. We fund research and advocacy efforts to equip the creative sector with messages and tools to help them expand their audiences, increase public funding, and move arts and culture higher on the policy agenda.  

Photo courtesy Seattle Art Museum

A dedicated funder of the arts in Washington state 

Investing in organizations spanning visual, literary, and performing arts, including new media and social practice arts

$30.8M active grants in arts & culture 

Funding the groups that provide essential artist services, strengthen artist and cultural networks, advocate for the wellbeing of artists and culture bearers, and help us all learn about diverse cultures and their ways of life.

Bolstering public sentiment for arts and culture in Washington

Contributing to the development of data and information to articulate the value of arts and culture, as well as unique sector needs.

A dedicated funder of the arts in Washington state 

Investing in organizations spanning visual, literary, and performing arts, including new media and social practice arts

$30.8M active grants in arts & culture 

Funding the groups that provide essential artist services, strengthen artist and cultural networks, advocate for the wellbeing of artists and culture bearers, and help us all learn about diverse cultures and their ways of life.

Bolstering public sentiment for arts and culture in Washington

Contributing to the development of data and information to articulate the value of arts and culture, as well as unique sector needs.

Photo courtesy Seattle Art Museum

Featured Grantees

Grantee: Pacific Northwest Ballet Association

Geography: Pacific Northwest

Funding: $1,250,000

Duration: 36 Months

This project will unify PNB’s emerging artist efforts into a singular choreographic dance program, RELEVÉ, with more technical training and performance opportunities, increasing engagement for young artists and expanding the Community Stages program.

Learn More

Grantee: Theatre Off Jackson

Geography: Pacific Northwest

Funding: $350,000

Duration: 36 Months

Theatre Off Jackson, a hub for diverse and affordable artistic performances based in Seattle, will boost staffing and make facility improvements to expand community engagement and better serve smaller arts groups and independent artists.

Learn More

Grantee: Seattle Symphony Orchestra

Geography: Pacific Northwest

Funding: $750,000

Duration: 36 Months

Public spaces at Benaroya Hall, home of the symphony, will be upgraded with new amenities and activated through public art displays, public programs, and community performances, to cultivate new and diverse audiences.

Learn More

Keep in Touch

We’re excited to connect with you and share regular updates across our arts and culture, environment, and youth programs. Sign up to hear about new RFPs, grantee news, and updates from the Allen Family Philanthropies team.
